P Mayor's Office Office of Sustainability Peace Commission Police Department Police Review and Advisory Board Public Health Public Works (DPW) Purchasing Q - Z Retirement Board School Department Tourism Office Transportation Veterans' Services Water Department Weights & Measures Women's Commission # 22-CityView Department Directory Home > I Want To > Get a Library Card Get a Library Card Cambridge Public Library Getting a library card is free and easy! Any resident of Massachusetts over the age of 5 can get a Cambridge Public Library card. When you have a library card, you can borrow books, movies, magazines, music, STEAM kits and many other items for free. To learn more about our borrowing periods, renewals, and fine free program, go to Borrowing Policies . How to Start Start by applying for a digital library card here . Visit any of the seven Cambridge Public Library locations with your photo identification and proof of address. (Children between the ages of 5 and 14 do not need to show identification or proof of address.) Your new card will be ready within minutes. Requirements To get a Cambridge Public Library card, you must: Live, work, go to school or own property in Massachusetts (for at least 2 months); Be at least 5 years old; and Register in person at any Cambridge Public Library location. If you don’t meet these requirements, you can still apply for a digital card through the Minuteman Library Network . This gives you instant access to digital books and audiobooks and some databases. View ID Requirements below. Contact Cambridge Public Library 617-349-4040 Email us Visit Website 449 Broadway Cambridge, MA 02138 Main Library Sun: 1PM - 5PM Mon - Thu: 9AM - 9PM Fri - Sat: 9AM - 5PM ID Requirements If you are over the age of 18: Please bring your Massachusetts Driver’s License or Massachusetts State ID. If you don’t have one of these, please bring one other form of photo identification: Passport Alien Resident Card University, School or Military ID EBT card Senior/TAP ID issued by the MBTA AND one of the following: Utility bill or tax bill (dated within the last 60 days)* Lease agreement* Imprinted bank check or deposit slip Official school schedule with applicant’s name and address typed on it* Official letter verifying residency and mailing address dated within last 30 days from a social service provider, temporary employer that provides housing, or a short-term residence A postmarked piece of mail or package *Electronic versions are acceptable. If you are between the ages of 14 and 18: Please show a school ID, health card, class schedule* or graded homework. *Electronic versions are acceptable. If you are under 14: You do not need to show photo identification or proof of address. About Your Library Card All Cambridge Public Library cards come with the same borrowing privileges: any patron can borrow up to 150 items at one time. (See Borrowing Policies to find out how long you can keep an item and how to request more time.) All Cambridge Public Library card accounts are individual and private. You are the only one who can use your library card. Cambridge Public Library staff will not share any of your account information (including what you have borrowed) with anyone unless you are there in person and give your permission. Put your Cambridge Public Library card on your smartphone! Download the free Minuteman Library Network app, type in your library card number and PIN and use the library from your phone. Contact City Hall 795 Massachusetts Ave. Cambridge , MA 02139 Ph: 617-349-4000 Hours of Service Mon: 8:30AM - 8PM Tue - Thu: 8:30AM - 5PM Fri: 8:30AM - 12PM Pay Bills Online Property Database Watch City Council Calendar View City Directory Find Forms & Documents Yi-An Huang, City Manager Public Records Request Disclaimer Jobs Privacy Statement Social Media Media Inquiries Contact Us © 2026 City of Cambridge Contact Us How can we help? Please provide as much detail below as possible so City staff can respond to your inquiry: Description: * Name: Email: As a governmental entity, the Massachusetts Public Records Law applies to records made or received by the City. Any information received through use of this site is subject to the same provisions as information provided on paper. Read our complete privacy statement Service Requests Enter a service request via SeeClickFix for things like missed trash pickups, potholes, etc., click here Close Original text Rate this translation Your feedback will be used to help improve Google Translate

Your browser has javascript disabled. Without javascript some functions will not work.
Book your museum passes with your Minuteman Library card here! Requirements:
Passes may be reserved up to 30 days in advance. Passes for the same museum may be reserved once every 30 days. Only one pass per household per day.
Digital passes will be emailed to you. Physical passes must be picked up at the library location you selected when making a reservation. 
More information: https://www.cambridgema.gov/Departments/cambridgepubliclibrary/iwantto/getamuseumpass
American Repertory Theater at Harvard University SELECT SHOWS - The A.R.T. makes passes available to select performances of select shows. Availability will be determined 30 days before the start of each performance's run. Next show: Black Swan, May 29 - June 28, 2026 Each pass admits 2 people for free for either a Friday 7:30 pm performance or a Sunday 2:00 pm performance. After booking, you will receive an email confirmation with...
Boston By Foot SEASONAL PASS - Each pass admits up to 1 adult or 1 child for free. Visit www.bostonbyfoot.org to browse Boston By Foot's extensive public tour calendar. Passes are valid for all public walking tours; they are not valid for private tours, water-based tours, or annual celebration tours (Beacon Hill with a BOO! & Footloose on the Freedom Trail).
Boston Children's Museum Each pass admits up to 4 (adults or children) at half price per person. Library Coupons are not valid for Sunday afternoons (Admission is $1 per person on Sundays from 1:30 p.m. to 4:30 p.m.). This pass is valid only on the date printed on the pass.
Boston Harbor Islands Ferry SEASONAL PASS - Each pass admits up to 4 people at 50% off. Summer 2026 schedule: Georges Island May 15 - June 14: Thursdays through Sundays June 16 - September 1: every day Spectacle Island July 15 - September 1: every day September 4 - 21: Thursdays through Sundays September 26 - October 13: Fridays through Sundays Peddocks Island from Hingham June 15 -...
Charles River Boat Tour SEASONAL PASS - Each pass admits 1 person for free. You must pick up your pass at the Main Library. 2026 Season Cruises daily May 22-September 7 Mon-Thurs at 12pm, 1:30pm and 3pm Fri-Sun at 12pm, 1:30pm, 3pm, and 4:30pm Cruises September 9-27 Wed-Thurs at 12pm, 1:30pm, and 3pm Fri-Sun at 12pm, 1:30pm, 3pm, and...
Harvard Museums of Science & Culture Each pass admits up to 4 people for free. Passes are valid for one-time use any day through the expiration date listed on the pass. Note: Harvard Museums of Science & Culture are free to Massachusetts residents every Sunday morning (year-round) from 9:00 am to 12:00 pm and on Wednesdays from 3:00 pm to 5:00 pm. Proof of residency required. This offer is not available to commercial groups.
How Do You See the World? Experience + Mapparium™ Each pass admits up to 4 adults free of charge to the Mapparium™ globe in the How Do You See the World? experience (value $6/Adult). Youth 17 and younger are always free. All other exhibits in the How Do You See the World? experience are always free, as are weekend church tours, and neither require a ticket. Pass instructions: Redeem this pass for tickets onsite in the Welcome...
Institute of Contemporary Art Each pass admits 2 people at $10 per person. People age 18 and under are admitted free. This pass is not date specific and may be used any time before the expiration date printed on the pass.
Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum Each pass admits up to 4 adults at $5 admission per person. Children under the age of 18 are always admitted for free.
Museum of African American History Each pass admits up to 4 people for free. Children 4 years and younger are admitted for free. Pass instructions: Visit the museum’s website and use the library coupon code emailed to you to reserve a timed entry Presently, online reservations are required for all visitors.
Museum of Fine Arts Each promo code may be redeemed for up to 2 adult tickets at $15 each (general admission and special exhibition) and up to 4 youth ages 7–17 at $10 each (special exhibition only). There is no discount for children 7–17 for general admission, currently $10. Children 6 and under are admitted free. Pass instructions: After reserving the library's pass, you will receive a code Use that...
Museum of Science Each pass admits 4 people free admission to the Exhibit Hall. Pass instructions: You will receive a pass with a QR code on it. You do not need to make an advance reservation. Bring your pass with the QR code on it with you when you visit the Museum. Digital or printed copies are accepted. You do not need to stop at the Box Office unless you are planning on purchasing tickets to any...
New England Aquarium Each pass admits up to 4 people for 50% off. Valid only for Aquarium admission. This coupon does not provide any other discounts and is not valid for movie or whale watch tickets. NOTE: Starting May 26, this pass admits up to 4 people for $20 per person and is only valid on weekdays. This coupon is valid only on the date printed on the pass.
Royall House & Slave Quarters SEASONAL PASS - Each pass admits 2 people for free for a 50 minute guided tour. Tours are at 1:00PM, 2:00PM, and 3:00PM. Visitors are invited to walk the grounds and view the exhibits before and after the tour. Children 12 and younger are admitted free. After reserving a library pass, you will receive an email confirmation with a promo code. Please arrive at least 15 minutes before the...
Trustees Go Pass SEASONAL PASS - Each pass admits 2 adults & children under 18 at the Family Level admission rate. Passes are not date specific and can be used once until the expiration date printed on the pass. Valid at more than 100 Trustees properties that collect an admission fee (including Fruitlands, deCordova and The Old Manse). Please note that some properties require pre-registration....
USS Constitution Museum Each pass allows free admission for up to 5 visitors per day. Limit one pass per party. Admission is first come first served, no advance tickets or reservations. Present printed pass or digital pass to Admissions. Valid only for the reserved date of visit. Ship is open Wednesday-Sunday 10am-4pm and closed Mondays and Tuesdays. Museum is open daily 10am-5pm.
Zoo New England Each pass admits up to 6 people to either the Franklin Park Zoo (Boston) or the Stone Zoo (Stoneham) at a discounted rate of $9 per adult and $6 per child.
